region_scope: Boston-Cambridge-Quincy, MA-NH HMFA (Massachusetts state sources only)
note: Where-to-obtain pointers for the document types the RealDoor checklist actually asks for. Every entry was opened in a browser on the retrieved date and reflects what that page states. Nothing here is an eligibility statement, a legal opinion, or an instruction about what any program requires - it answers only 'who issues this document and where does the applicant go to get it.' Timeframes and fees appear ONLY where the official page states them; absent fields mean the page was silent, not that the answer is zero. State-specific entries (authority 'official_ma_state') are correct for Massachusetts and must not be shown to users outside MA. malegislature.gov was unreachable from the retrieval network, so statute text is cited via the Mass.gov guide that quotes it rather than via the statute URL; those statute citations sit in source_locator and are marked statute_url_verified false.

sources.how_to_obtain: Sign in to your my Social Security account and download the benefit verification letter as a PDF, or call SSA and say "proof of income" when prompted.
sources.timeframe_stated: Online: the page states you can "Download a PDF to get the letter right away."
sources.source_locator: Sections 'Sign in to your account' and the call-us block; intro paragraph notes the letter "may be referred to as a 'proof of income letter' or 'benefit letter'".

sources.how_to_obtain: Log in to DTA Connect (mobile app or DTAConnect.com) and request a letter showing the amount of benefits you receive, or call the DTA Assistance Line and request it through the automated prompts.
sources.source_locator: DTA Connect capability list: "Request a letter showing the amount of benefits you receive (income verification letter)"; same item appears in the DTA Assistance Line list as "(benefit verification letter)".
sources.applies_to_programs: SNAP, TAFDC, EAEDC cases administered by DTA

sources.issued_by: Massachusetts State Supplement Program (SSP), administered by DTA
sources.how_to_obtain: Request the SSP benefit verification letter through the online benefit verification request form, or call the SSP Assistance Line; if you receive SSP plus other DTA benefits, call the DTA Assistance Line instead.
sources.timeframe_stated: "You should receive the benefit verification letter approximately 10 days after you request it."
sources.source_locator: Sections 'What you need', 'How to request' (By phone / Online), and 'Next steps' -> 'When will I get my benefit verification letter?'
sources.note: The letter is mailed, so the page carries a standing notice to make sure your mailing address is current.

sources.how_to_obtain: Log in to your Unemployment Services for Workers account to view your claim status, determination messages and benefit payment information.
sources.source_locator: 'How to check' -> "Log in to your Unemployment Services for Workers account"; claim status definitions list (Monetarily Eligible / Determined Eligible / Ready to Request Benefits).
sources.note: The page describes viewing claim and payment status in the portal. It does not state that a standalone printable benefit letter is produced; do not promise one.

sources.how_to_obtain: For prior-year unemployment income, log in to your Unemployment Services for Workers account and download your Form 1099-G, or call the DUA 1099-G request line.
sources.timeframe_stated: "DUA mails 1099-G forms by the end of January for the previous year"; "Forms for the previous year will be available by the end of January of the year after you collect benefits."
sources.source_locator: Sections 'What you need' and 'How to request' (Online / By phone); Contact block 'Unemployment benefits 1099-G request line'.
sources.note: 1099-G is an annual tax form covering a past calendar year, not a statement of current monthly benefits.

sources.how_to_obtain: Ask your employer or payroll department for the pay statements they are required to issue with your pay; in Massachusetts the employer may not charge you for them, and if they are provided electronically the employer must give you a way to print them for free.
sources.fee_stated: "Employers may not charge workers for paystubs."
sources.source_locator: Section 'Paystubs' - lists required contents (employer and worker name, hours worked, hourly rate, deductions and increases) and the no-charge / free-printing rule. Guide cites M.G.L. c. 149, sec. 148.

sources.how_to_obtain: If pay statements are missing, ask the employer to let you see your payroll record - in Massachusetts workers have the right to see their own payroll records at reasonable times and places, and employers must keep them for 3 years.
sources.source_locator: Section 'Payroll records' - "Employers must keep payroll records for 3 years... Workers have the right to see their own payroll records at reasonable times and places." Guide cites M.G.L. c. 151, sec. 15.
sources.note: A right to SEE the record is not stated as a right to a copy. Do not tell the user they will receive a document from this route.

sources.how_to_obtain: An employment verification letter is written by your employer - there is no government office that issues one. If the employer does not respond, Massachusetts workers may submit a written request for their personnel record, which the employer must let them review or provide a copy of within five business days.
sources.timeframe_stated: "within five business days of a written request" (personnel record route only)
sources.source_locator: Section 'Personnel records' - "Employers who keep personnel records must allow workers to review their own personnel records or receive a copy of their personnel files within five business days of a written request." Guide cites M.G.L. c. 149, sec. 52C.
sources.note: A personnel record is NOT an employment verification letter. This is a fallback for obtaining employment documentation when an employer is unresponsive, and must be labeled as such in any UI.

sources.how_to_obtain: Sign in to your IRS Individual Online Account and open Tax Records -> transcripts to view, print or download your Wage and Income Transcript, which shows the Forms W-2, 1099 and 1098 series reported to the IRS for you.
sources.fee_stated: Free (the transcript itself; see DOC-SRC-011 for the paid full-return copy).
sources.source_locator: 'Access tax records and transcripts in your Individual Online Account' and 'Alternative to getting a transcript online' (5-10 calendar days, 800-908-9946); Topic 159 states the wage and income transcript can be requested via the online account or Form 4506-T and covers the past ten tax years.
sources.note: Topic 159 also states current-year information may be incomplete until earnings are reported, and that transcripts partially mask personally identifiable information while leaving financial data visible.

sources.issued_by: Digital platform (the app or website the work is booked through)
sources.how_to_obtain: Earnings statements and information returns for gig work come from the digital platform itself - check the earnings or tax-documents section of the platform account. The IRS gig economy tax center identifies which forms a platform may issue (Form 1099-K, 1099-MISC, 1099-NEC).
sources.source_locator: Sections 'Gig economy income is taxable' (form types listed), 'What is gig work?' and 'What are digital platforms?'. Page last reviewed 09-Jul-2026.
sources.note: No government portal issues a gig earnings statement. Per-platform procedures differ and no specific platform is named here by design; the federal cross-check for gig income is DOC-SRC-009.

sources.how_to_obtain: If you need annual wage figures rather than individual pay statements, request the free Wage and Income Transcript; an actual copy of a filed Form W-2 requires requesting the full tax return with Form 4506.
sources.fee_stated: $30 per return for a copy of the full tax return via Form 4506 (waived for taxpayers affected by a federally declared disaster who include the designation). The transcript itself is free.
sources.source_locator: Topic no. 159 - transcript vs. actual W-2 copy; Form 4506 fee paragraph.
sources.note: The IRS states it only retains actual W-2 copies when attached to a paper return.

gaps.reason: The application summary is generated by the leasing or property management office handling the specific application. No federal, Massachusetts state, or Boston-area government portal was found that issues it, and none should be invented. The correct instruction is to ask the property that took the application.

Employment verification letters are authored by the employer. No official page describes a procedure for obtaining one; only the Massachusetts personnel-record fallback (DOC-SRC-008) is source-backed.

Retrieval steps live inside each platform's own account UI. Naming or describing specific platforms would be unsourced and would amount to recommending private companies, so only the IRS framing (DOC-SRC-010) and the IRS transcript cross-check (DOC-SRC-009) are recorded.

There is no single benefit letter. The issuing agency depends on which program pays the benefit (SSA, DTA, SSP, DUA). The applicant must pick their own agency; the tool must not guess which one applies to them.

Connections to malegislature.gov timed out from the retrieval network on 2026-07-19. Statute text is therefore cited through the Mass.gov guide that restates it, and every statute_url carries statute_url_verified false. Re-verify before presenting statute URLs as checked.
